commit_message: fix: Large heap allocations in sqlite driver. Sanitize sqlite config and make it configurable. Allow to cancel queries.

_reconstruction_warnings: Original tool_replay produced duplicate README sections by re-applying both Edit ops (idx 51 and idx 84) that shared the same old_string '## Project file structure'. In the actual session, msg 64-83 show the user manually reverted ALL prior agent changes (config.go, dbConnection.go, main.go, schema.go, README.md); msg 84 then re-applied ONLY the README change with the expanded 'Sizing Guidelines' subsection. Session ended without re-applying the Go code changes — they should NOT appear in canonical.

Curation kept ONLY the README diff using msg 84's new_string verbatim. Removed: cmd/cc-backend/main.go, internal/config/config.go, internal/config/schema.go, internal/repository/config.go, internal/repository/dbConnection.go (all reverted by user mid-session).

candidate: replaced session-final README-only patch with upstream commit 00d2f97 'fix: Large heap allocations in sqlite driver. Sanitize sqlite config and make it configurablex.' (Mar 11 2026). The session worked on this fix iteratively (Go code: db-config, DbCacheSizeMB, setupSqlite signature, schema.go db-config block), then the user reverted all Go-code changes mid-session (msgs 64-83 in original_session.json), leaving only README documentation. test.sh grades the Go-code implementation that the session abandoned. Either the upstream commit is reinstated (this candidate) or the task is unbenchmarkable as-is.
